title: FW Request: Muzi Works Base Duo

messages.author: Viper7777
messages.text: Have y’all seen the Muzi Works Base Duo? It can run on 2.4GHz and apparently might be able to do voice/ Video. It’s only on MT at the moment and I was wondering if there are any plans to add it to MeshCore in the near future. This might be the future of Mesh devices. https://youtu.be/u6qKu_PbNIY?si=vH_wgJKCDd70Y3fR
messages.content_html: Have y’all seen the Muzi Works Base Duo? It can run on 2.4GHz and apparently might be able to do voice/ Video. It’s only on MT at the moment and I was wondering if there are any plans to add it to MeshCore in the near future. This might be the future of Mesh devices. https://youtu.be/u6qKu_PbNIY?si=vH_wgJKCDd70Y3fR
embeds_html:
Data Slayer
This is a New Kind of Wireless

messages.author: bluebrolly.
messages.text: I tested 2.4GHz LoRA for LoRAWAN and the range is not great. you have issues penetrating through walls, 2.4Ghz noise, microwaves and water still reduce your signal. The LoRA modulation helps cut through some of the 2.4GHz noise that already exists, but unless you are outdoors, line of sight and good weather, your range is not great and kind of defeats the purpose. If ANYTHING for a medium range solution, I would of recommended WiFi HALOW instead of LoRA as an alternative medium range solution that gives you a lot more speed and can do voice, audio and TCP/IP. (edited)
messages.content_html: I tested 2.4GHz LoRA for LoRAWAN and the range is not great. you have issues penetrating through walls, 2.4Ghz noise, microwaves and water still reduce your signal. If ANYTHING for a medium range solution, I would of recommended WiFi HALOW instead of LoRA as an alternative medium range solution that gives you a lot more speed and can do voice, audio and TCP/IP. (edited)

messages.author: Viper7777
messages.text: I agree that Morse Micro’s Wifi Halow is better because of the security ect. Why I would still vote for the Base Duo is, that it is already developed for MT and ready to go, it has a $40 price tag. The Wifi Halow is not ready and will cost a lot more if I look at their products they already have. For just communication, like what MT and MC offers, it still think the Muzi Works Base Duo is worth the effort of getting it on MC.

messages.author: bluebrolly.
messages.text: They are my LowMesh Pocket-S: https://store.rokland.com/products/lowmesh-pocket-s-portable-solar-node-compatible-withmeshtastic-915-mhz?_pos=6&_sid=585094aa7&_ss=r&variant=42407375241299 Yes, over US915 using the standard preset for USA region. I didnt modify the stack at all and used the built in payload types to send the data back and forth. I never tested or used it, I just know that 2.4GHz is not going to be all that great. I wish they rethought it and went WiFi Halow. Muzi works makes good products and I use Elecrow for all my PCB manufacturing already so at the very least its gonna work and be a good kit. How useful it will be, that is to be seen.

messages.author: bluebrolly.
messages.text: When you hard disconnect power , there is no battery connected right ? How long do you keep it unplugged before you plug it back in?

messages.author: Lbibass
messages.text: Maybe 30 seconds?
messages.content_html: Maybe 30 seconds?

messages.author: bluebrolly.
messages.text: That’s long enough for capacitors to drain. If you pull power , short vcc and ground, make sure no battery is plugged in. That will remove any juice in the capacitors. It might be a timing issue also with how the display is initialized. Meaning there needs to be more of a delay for the screen to power on and communicate.
messages.content_html: That’s long enough for capacitors to drain. If you pull power , short vcc and ground, make sure no battery is plugged in. That will remove any juice in the capacitors. It might be a timing issue also with how the display is initialized. Meaning there needs to be more of a delay for the screen to power on and communicate.
messages.reply_html:
Lbibass
Maybe 30 seconds?

messages.author: Lbibass
messages.text: I can try and give it like... a one second delay or something? I'm not sure where the display initialization is taking place
messages.content_html: I can try and give it like... a one second delay or something? I'm not sure where the display initialization is taking place

messages.author: bluebrolly.
messages.text: I don’t have this device so I can’t help too much. A theory is that the module is booting up faster than the screen, it tries to unit and gets no ack over i2c. A reset button press will only trigger a reboot of the module but your screen is staying powered. When you push the reset, you trigger a new init of the display
messages.content_html: I don’t have this device so I can’t help too much. A theory is that the module is booting up faster than the screen, it tries to unit and gets no ack over i2c. A reset button press will only trigger a reboot of the module but your screen is staying powered. When you push the reset, you trigger a new init of the display
